title: express-session with cookie.secure: false — the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP and can be sniffed

params.passDetail: express-session sets cookie.secure to true — the session cookie is only ever transmitted over HTTPS.
params.failDetail: express-session is configured with cookie: { secure: false }, so the session cookie is sent over plain HTTP as well as HTTPS. On any non-TLS hop an attacker can read the session id and hijack the authenticated session. Set cookie.secure: true in production (with app.set('trust proxy', 1) when TLS terminates at a proxy).
params.absentCallDetail: This scope does not call session(); this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: session() does not set cookie.secure; verify production forces HTTPS-only cookies (secure: true).

provenance.note: cookie: { secure: false } is copied from local-dev config (where there's no HTTPS) into production, sending the session cookie in the clear. Distinct from the saveUninitialized rule; this is the nested cookie-flag footgun the nested-path checker now reaches.
